records.ety: [L. ascendere; ad + scandere to climb, mount. See Scan.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Ascended; p. pr. & vb. n. Ascending.]

defs: To move upward; to mount; to go up; to rise; -- opposed to descend.
quotes: I ascend unto my father and your father.
The smoke of it ascended up to heaven.

defs: To rise, in a figurative sense; to proceed from an inferior to a superior degree, from mean to noble objects, from particulars to generals, from modern to ancient times, from one note to another more acute, etc.; as, our inquiries ascend to the remotest antiquity; to ascend to our first progenitor.
To go or move upward upon or along; to climb; to mount; to go up the top of; as, to ascend a hill, a ladder, a tree, a river, a throne.
